key_insight: Ultrasound-driven bubbles can penetrate and alter dense fibrin networks, improving drug delivery in chronic blood clots.
engineering_relevance: The study highlights a novel approach to enhance thrombolytic therapy through acoustic cavitation mechanics.
method: Using ultrasound-driven microbubbles to create three-dimensional paths in fibrin networks.
Evaluating the impact of radial bubble oscillation on fibrin fiber mechanics.
Investigating the softening behavior of fibrin networks under various peak force conditions.
evidence_signal: Repeated bubble oscillations lead to plastic deformation and damage accumulation in fibrin networks over thousands of cycles.
watch_item: Further research is needed to understand the long-term effects of repeated bubble oscillations on fibrin network integrity.
paper_title: Interaction mechanics of acoustic cavitation with fibrin networks
